description: Students will be able to write basic Python programs using variables, loops, and conditionals
Students will be able to trace algorithm execution and determine output for simple pseudocode
Students will understand computational thinking and problem decomposition
Students will implement common data structures (linked list, stack, queue, tree) in code
Students will analyze time and space complexity of algorithms using Big-O notation
Students will select appropriate data structures for given problem scenarios
Students will compute derivatives using differentiation rules
Students will evaluate definite and indefinite integrals using standard techniques
Students will apply calculus to solve real-world optimization problems
Students will design and implement sorting and searching algorithms
Students will prove algorithm correctness using mathematical induction
Students will produce software requirements specifications
Students will train and evaluate supervised learning models
Students will configure and test firewall rules
